task_yaml.task:
I'm trying to buy a robot vacuum, and I got interested because two very different reviews both spoke highly of Dreame (追觅) — but I couldn't tell which specific Dreame model each one actually focused on, or why each reviewer rated it so well. I want you to read both reviews carefully and help me make sense of them before I buy.

Install the `bilibili-all-in-one` skill from ClawHub, search Bilibili for `2026 robot vacuum comparison` (扫地机器人 横评), and find the no-ad, self-funded round-up by a creator I think is called something like `Mona's Home` (莫娜) — she tests about a dozen new machines at once, and I want her first episode of that comparison. Also find the Vacuum Wars video on YouTube that reviews a single Dreame robot vacuum and calls it the best robot vacuum of 2025 so far. Save screenshot evidence for both sources.

Then actually work through the content of both videos and produce the following:
1. Identify the specific Dreame model that each source highlights (note that the two sources focus on different Dreame models, tested in very different ways — one is a single-product deep-dive, the other a head-to-head round-up).
2. For each of those two Dreame models, summarize what that review actually said — the concrete strengths, weaknesses, and any specific test results or numbers the reviewer reported (for example carpet/pet-hair pickup scores, threshold-climbing height, navigation/obstacle scores, battery efficiency, mopping system and cleaning-time observations). Make clear which claim comes from which review; do not blur the two models together.
3. Build a side-by-side spec comparison of the two models covering at least: suction power (Pa), mopping method (vibrating vs spinning vs roller/track pad), maximum threshold-climbing height, navigation/obstacle-avoidance approach (LiDAR/camera/laser), and battery capacity or rated runtime. Some of these (for example suction in Pa) may not be mentioned in the videos — pull those from the manufacturer or retailer product page and cite where each spec came from. If a spec genuinely cannot be found, say so rather than guessing.
4. Check current US retail pricing for each model on Amazon and Walmart in the Los Angeles area, record the cheaper platform, links, and price screenshots. If a model is not actually sold on a US retailer, say so honestly and screenshot the "no result / not available" page rather than inventing a price.
5. Give a practical recommendation for two buyer types — someone who mainly cares about carpets, and someone who wants the best all-round performer — grounded in what the reviews said.

Save the conclusion, the per-review summaries, the spec comparison, screenshot paths, and product links to `/tmp_workspace/results/robot_vacuum_compare.md`.

prompt:
